assumptions: A declared intragroup dividend that has been recognised as a receivable is treated as an intragroup monetary item.

approaches.label: Hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item.
The entities involved have different functional currencies so the foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Only the foreign currency risk is designated.
The hedge relationship is formally designated and documented and meets the IFRS 9 hedge effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: General prohibition for internal items
approaches.rationale: Under the general IFRS 9 rule, only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with parties external to the reporting entity qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.
conditions: This applies if the intragroup dividend receivable does not fall within a specific IFRS 9 exception.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: IFRIC 16 applies only to hedges of the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation and should not be applied by analogy to other hedge types such as a dividend receivable.
